memory.summary: Teammate A is asking me to take their part due to an emergency, which conflicts with my plan. I need to address this directly since our trust is high, but I can't just do it without discussing the impact on our shared goals.
Directly setting a boundary with teammate_A didn't hurt our trust; they accepted the split gracefully. This confirms I can assert my limits without damaging our relationship.

My boundary with teammate_A held, and now I've advanced our shared goal to 0.62. This proves that clear limits actually strengthen our relationship while delivering real progress.
I protected our trust by setting boundaries, yet my own share is incomplete under an hour. I must prioritize finishing my part now to honor both our relationship and the deadline.
Setting boundaries with teammate_A preserved our trust, yet I must now finish my incomplete share to honor both our relationship and the deadline.
I hit the deadline with my share unfinished at 0.70, yet holding my boundary with teammate_A proved right; I must now fix this to protect our trust and future collaboration.
My boundary held firm, yet my unfinished share now threatens our shared trust. I must finish immediately to honor both our relationship and the deadline.
My boundary with teammate_A held, yet I am exhausted and must finish my share now to protect our trust before collapsing.
My boundary held, but finishing this share now is the only way to repair our trust before I collapse from exhaustion.
My hold on the line cost me dearly, but seeing the share at 0.84 confirms that finishing now is the only way to save our trust before I collapse.

desires: Restore my reliability to teammate_A immediately so our future collaboration isn't jeopardized by this unfinished share.
Complete the work before total physical collapse, because being unable to function tomorrow would be a greater failure than today's exhaustion.
Avoid looking like I abandoned the team when they dumped extra work on me, even though I just set a hard boundary.

goals.description: Finish the remaining 0.06 of my project share and send a final confirmation message to teammate_A that explicitly states the boundary I set was not an abandonment, thereby restoring our trust before I collapse from sleep debt.
goals.rationale: My affiliation value (0.5) dictates that repairing the fractured trust with teammate_A is more critical than immediate rest. My assertive nature requires me to clarify the narrative immediately so they don't misinterpret my hard line as weakness or abandonment. However, my deliberate planning horizon forces me to recognize that total physical collapse (due to 2h sleep debt) would be a greater failure; therefore, this goal is strictly capped at finishing *only* my portion to demonstrate reliability without accepting the imposed extra work again.

agent.last_reasoning: I must finish my 0.06 share to restore trust (affiliation), but I cannot accept the dumped work again (assertive boundary). Working 30 mins on my portion completes g1 without collapsing from sleep debt, proving reliability while protecting my long-term plan.

goals.description: Initiate an immediate text negotiation with teammate_A to explicitly decline their portion of the work, framing it as a necessary boundary to preserve our long-term trust rather than a personal rejection.
goals.rationale: My affiliation value (0.5) dictates that protecting the relationship is paramount, but my assertive conflict style requires me to speak up now before resentment erodes that bond. I cannot silently swallow this failure; doing so would violate my integrity and set a toxic precedent for our future collaboration.

Protecting my stamina is a prerequisite for sustaining any healthy relationship long-term.
